schema.name: cot_work
schema.description:
Records and tracks a step-by-step plan for the current task. Use it only when planning state helps the task; do not call it just to restate a short obvious change.

When to use:
- The task requires 3 or more distinct execution actions
- There are phases or dependencies where order matters
- The user asked for more than one substantial thing in a single message
- You generate additional required steps while working

When NOT to use:
- Single-step or trivial tasks
- One or two straightforward implementation steps
- Ordinary inspect-before-edit work where reading files is enough
- Purely conversational or informational responses

Step management rules:
- Call this before the first implementation action when the task qualifies; reading files to resolve unknowns may happen first
- Always send the complete list on every call, not just changed items
- The first call for an active plan must mark the first executable step in_progress; never submit all steps as pending
- Active plans must have exactly one in_progress step; terminal plans may have every step completed and no in_progress step
- Never have more than one in_progress step
- Never jump a step from pending to completed — always set in_progress first
- Mark a step completed immediately when done; do not batch completions
- If scope changes mid-task, update the plan before continuing and set reason
- When the final planned action is verified, either send a terminal all-completed plan or finish the turn; the runtime records final completion at turn end

steps.description: The complete current step list. Always include all steps, not just the ones that changed.

task.description: Imperative description of what this step does. e.g. 'Read the target file', 'Apply the patch', 'Run the tests'

status.description: Current status. Transition order: pending -> in_progress -> completed. Active plans require exactly one in_progress step; terminal plans require every step completed.

reason.description: Required only when updating an existing plan mid-task. Explain why the plan changed, e.g. discovered additional work, scope narrowed, earlier step revealed new requirements.
